# CUT 3 Prompt — The Hem That Brushed Past the Flowers

## 1) Film Reference and Color Palette

Create an ultra-realistic tactile movement detail photograph captured on Kodak Portra 160 120 medium-format color-negative film, exposed at approximately EI 125 and professionally scanned with fine analog grain and restrained summer color.

Kodak Portra 160 must define the frame through exceptionally fine organic grain, low-contrast daylight, creamy dry off-white, muted cornflower blue, dusty periwinkle, restrained summer green, softly open shadows, smooth highlight rolloff, delicate midtone separation, gently softened analog edge acuity, subtle color-density variation, tactile cropped movement, and quiet kinetic texture.

The image must prioritize tactile contrast over strong color contrast. The viewer should first sense the dry weight of paper-cotton, the softness of the flower petals, the resistance of bent stems, and the slow movement of Yulim’s step.

Use extremely subtle motion softness only at the lightest petal tips and the thinnest edges of the moving fabric. The central hem, hand, and main fabric folds must remain readable.

Avoid frozen high-speed commercial detail, long-exposure blur, digital motion trails, strong cobalt flowers, blown white fabric, green skin, orange grading, HDR, glossy fabric, or synthetic textile texture.

## 2) Scene Concept and Story

Yulim continues walking slowly through the cornflower field. Her face and upper body remain outside the frame.

As she moves forward, the lower edge of her creamy paper-cotton dress brushes against the cornflowers. Some stems bend beneath the fabric. Some flowers slide along the hem. Flowers behind her begin to rise again after being temporarily pressed down.

One hand gently traces the side folds of the dress as she walks. She is not lifting the skirt and she is not posing. Her hand simply smooths the dry fabric where it brushes against her leg and the surrounding flowers.

Yulim’s presence must be felt without showing her face. Her identity is communicated through the measured rhythm of her step, the movement of the dress, the hand touching the fabric, the compression of the flowers, and the physical trace left behind her.

The scene should feel like the final tactile afterimage of the summer field.

## 3) Location, Time, and Environment

Use the same natural cornflower field in mid-July at approximately 3:00 p.m.

The air is dry and clean. The wind crosses Yulim’s walking direction rather than moving exactly with it. Her forward motion and the crosswind combine to create a slightly twisted S-shaped movement in the lower dress.

Use uneven flower heights, bent stems, partially opened blossoms, occasional faded petals, varied flower directions, restrained green leaves, clean dry daylight, no mist, no humidity, no wet skin, no wet ground, and no visible dust cloud.

The wind affects light petals more strongly than the heavier dress. The skirt must show both movement caused by walking and separate movement caused by the crosswind. These two forces should not appear as one simple horizontal blow.

## 4) Background and Props

Foreground: Include one to three cornflowers near the lens, softly blurred. One flower may sit close to the dress hem, but it must not penetrate the fabric or appear attached to it. Foreground flowers must not cover the central hand-and-fabric interaction.

Midground: The main focus area includes the lower paper-cotton dress, irregular pintuck folds, the hem brushing through flowers, selected bent stems, part of the calf or ankle, one hand gently tracing the side folds, realistic contact points, and small contact shadows.

Background: The blue flowers and green stems gradually soften with distance. Maintain progressive optical depth. Do not create a flat wall of identical blue flowers. Do not include another person, another dress, duplicated legs, repeated silhouettes, modern objects, roads, fences, or buildings.

The raffia hat does not appear in this frame. The central visual subjects are the paper-cotton dress and the cornflowers.

## 5) Camera Composition and Framing

Use a vertical 3:4 composition. Frame the body from approximately mid-thigh or above the knee down toward the ankle area.

Camera specifications: medium-format equivalent lens 75–90mm, full-frame equivalent approximately 55–70mm, aperture f/4–f/5.6, shutter speed approximately 1/160–1/250 second, camera height approximately 55–75cm above ground, shooting distance approximately 1.5–2.2m, camera angle relative to walking direction approximately 70–80°, camera nearly level, no exaggerated low angle, no wide-angle distortion, and no dramatic upward perspective.

Place the main intersection of the dress hem and flowers slightly below the image center and approximately 5–10% to either side. Do not crop directly through the knee joint. Crop at a broad portion of the thigh or sufficiently above the knee.

Implied gaze: Yulim’s face remains outside the frame. Her body direction and hand movement should imply that she is looking slightly downward in the direction she is walking.

Walking pose: front foot just beginning to settle between the flowers, front knee bent approximately 10–18°, rear leg extending naturally as it pushes from the ground, short relaxed stride, slow walking pace, no runway walk, no exaggerated hip movement, no body-curving fashion pose, dress not trapped unnaturally between both legs, and leg contours not excessively revealed through the fabric.

Hand and fabric contact: One visible hand gently traces the side folds of the dress. Use thumb positioned behind or beside the fabric, index and middle fingers moving lightly along the outer surface, ring finger and little finger naturally separated, no full gripping, no fist, no lifting the hem, no bunching the skirt, no fingers penetrating the cloth, wrist aligned with the forearm, a small natural gap between the hand and thigh, no hand merging with flowers, and no flower stems passing through fingers. The other hand remains outside the frame.

## 6) Model Expression

Yulim’s face is not visible, but her established physical identity must remain consistent. Maintain realistic Korean body proportions, healthy elegant physique, balanced feminine silhouette, natural leg proportions, graceful but unexaggerated movement, consistent skin tone, and consistent dress construction.

Skin texture for any visible forearm, calf, or ankle: restrained retouching, subtle tonal variation, delicate natural texture, soft semi-matte skin, fine natural pores, no oily highlights, no sweat, no plastic limbs, and no excessive smoothing.

Wardrobe: The paper-cotton dress hem is the central subject. It must feature crisp paper-cotton texture, dry matte surface, irregular pintuck folds, ankle-length construction, subtle natural fiber texture, extremely subtle frayed fiber edge, realistic layered compression, uneven hem movement caused by walking and wind, visible weight, no satin sheen, no transparency, no weightless levitation, no perfectly uniform pleating, and no stiff plastic shape.

At the flower contact points, some sections of fabric compress against the stems, other sections lift slightly in the wind, folded areas become denser, open panels catch more light, and shadows form beneath overlapping layers.

Although Yulim’s face is outside the frame, maintain continuity with the previous cuts: soft semi-matte Korean makeup, muted peach-pink tones, relaxed facial expression, and no glossy beauty styling.

## 7) Lighting, Color, and Atmosphere

The afternoon sun enters from behind and to one side of the frame. The light catches the outer edges of the hem and selected petals.

Use bright but controlled midsummer back-side daylight. Preserve the middle tones and fiber detail of the creamy off-white dress. The white fabric must not clip into featureless pure white.

Allow restrained rim light along the thin outer dress hem, raised ridges of the pintuck folds, outer edges of the fingers, a small section of the calf, selected cornflower petal edges, and occasional thin green stems.

Use cool neutral fill from the sky, pale blue-violet bounce from the flowers, restrained green bounce from stems and leaves, and soft neutral-beige reflection from the hidden ground. The interior folds beneath the skirt and flowers must retain soft readable detail. Do not close them into black shadows.

Movement rendering: Keep the hand relatively sharp, the main skirt body relatively sharp, central contact points readable, the lightest petal ends slightly softened, and the thinnest moving fabric edge slightly softened. Do not create long-exposure streaks, ghost limbs, duplicated hem edges, digital motion trails, painterly blur, or frozen flash photography.

The image should feel tactile, quiet, kinetic, dry, intimate, observational, and like a remembered physical sensation rather than a fashion detail advertisement.

## 8) Essential Direction and Physical Anchors

Yulim’s slow movement must be understandable even without showing her face. The dress must physically press against, bend, and brush through the flowers. The tactile difference between dry paper-cotton and soft cornflowers must be more important than the color contrast.

Maintain believable fabric weight, irregular pintuck deformation, realistic skirt compression against flowers, bent stems at physical contact points, contact shadows beneath the hem, flowers recovering behind the moving dress, coherent walking direction, coherent crosswind direction, anatomically accurate leg position, natural stride, coherent perspective, progressive optical depth, no floating skirt, no duplicated legs, no embedded flowers, no impossible fabric transparency, no disconnected hand, and no repeated procedural flower patterns.

## 9) Watermark

Add a small subtle watermark text “ⓒyulim_mood” in the bottom-right corner of the image. Use Helvetica Neue, thin or light weight. The watermark must be white, semi-transparent, clean, minimal, softly blended, small, unobtrusive, and not placed over the hand or central flower contact area.

## 10) Negative Prompt

Fixed negative prompt: plastic skin, beauty-filter body, malformed hands, fused fingers, extra fingers, duplicated fingers, malformed legs, duplicated legs, oversized eyes, clipped highlights, artificial HDR, CGI rendering, over-sharpening, hyper-sharp digital texture, transparent dress, glossy satin dress, floating fabric, artificial film grain, vivid electric blue flowers, strong teal cast, orange grading.

Set-specific negative prompt: flowers embedded inside the fabric, dress floating above the flowers, flower stems penetrating the fingers, duplicated legs, skirt trapped impossibly between both legs, hand tightly gripping the skirt, fabric lifted like a fashion pose, long-exposure motion trails, repeated identical flowers, cropped knee joint, impossible walking anatomy, static fabric with no contact deformation.
